title: From a blank page
testCase: A whole site: semantics, media, a table, a form, a viewport.
blocks.0.text: An empty editor. The last time.
blocks.1.text: No instructions. No steps. No hints about how many things to include.
blocks.3.text: Build the site for your topic. The whole thing. The kind you would show someone.
blocks.6.text: I will not list the tags for you. If you know them, you will use them. If not — go back and look.
blocks.7.text: But I will remind you of one thing: you are not the person who opened this course. That person typed "Hello" into an empty file and wondered what had happened.
blocks.9.text: You will freeze. A blank file does that to everyone, including people who have done this for twenty years.
blocks.10.text: The difference is that you now know what to do with the feeling. You do not wait for it to pass. You write one tag. Any tag. Then the next.
blocks.11.text: Nobody sits down and writes a whole site. Everyone writes one tag at a time until it becomes one.
blocks.13.text: For the last time: it will be ugly. Serif type, one column, no colours, the image too big.
blocks.14.text: And for the last time: that is not your fault. You wrote the skeleton. The skin comes in the next course.
blocks.15.text: But think about what is under the ugliness. A page Google understands. That a blind person can read. That fills in with a keyboard. That says what each thing IS, not how it looks.
blocks.16.text: There are people who have written HTML for years and cannot do that. You can.
blocks.18.text: When you are done, do this: copy your code, save it as index.html on your own computer, and open it with a double click.
blocks.19.text: It will open in a real browser. Not a preview, not a lesson. Your file, on your machine, in the real world.
blocks.20.text: That is the moment. From here on you do not need a course to make things.
